source: physical-trainer knowledge base v1 — spine-conscious framework (no axial loading, no loaded spinal flexion)

contraindication_tags.axial_load: compresses the spine along its axis (bar on back, shoulder pads, heavy carries)
contraindication_tags.loaded_spinal_flexion: flexes the spine against resistance (crunches under load)
contraindication_tags.unsupported_hinge: loaded hip hinge with an unsupported torso (deadlifts, bent rows)

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.cue: Shoulder blades pinned to the bench, feet planted; bar touches the lower chest with forearms vertical.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.cue: Let the dumbbells drift slightly apart at the bottom — feel the stretch across the chest, not the shoulder.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.cue: 30-45° bench; press up and slightly back so the load stays over the upper chest.

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Press
exercises.cue: Bar to the clavicle line; elbows about 45° from the torso.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.cue: Set the seat so the handles line up with mid-chest; drive through without shrugging.

exercises.name: Cable Fly
exercises.cue: Arc the hands like hugging a barrel; the stretch at the back is the working part — don't rush it.

exercises.name: Pec Deck Fly
exercises.cue: Elbows slightly bent and fixed; squeeze for a full second at the front.

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.cue: Rigid plank from head to heel; the chest leads, the hips follow.

exercises.name: Weighted Dip
exercises.cue: A slight forward lean shifts the work to the chest; stop at shoulder-level depth.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.cue: Pull the elbows to the hips, not the chin to the bar.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.cue: Supinated grip; drive the elbows down and back.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.cue: Chest tall, bar to the collarbone; resist the weight on the way up for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Neutral-Grip Pulldown
exercises.cue: The neutral grip biases the lats with friendlier shoulders; same cue — elbows to hips.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.cue: Arms nearly straight; sweep the bar to the thighs using the lats, not the triceps.

exercises.name: Machine Pullover
exercises.cue: Big stretch overhead, then drive the elbows down toward the sides.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row
exercises.cue: Chest glued to the pad — that's what keeps the spine out of it; squeeze the blades together.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
exercises.cue: Incline bench, chest on the pad; row to the hip line.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.cue: Torso upright and still; let the blades protract fully, then row without leaning back.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.cue: Hand and knee braced on the bench keeps the spine supported; row the elbow to the hip.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row (Chest-Supported)
exercises.cue: Full stretch at the bottom, drive through the elbows.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.cue: A loaded hinge held under fatigue — swap for a chest-supported row to train the same muscles without the spinal demand.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.cue: Rope to the bridge of the nose, thumbs pointing back; finish like a double-biceps pose.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.cue: Straight up and slightly back; hold the top for a beat and keep the load moderate.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Back supported on the bench — that support is what makes this the spine-friendly press; press to lockout without flaring the ribs.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Handles start at ear height; drive up without shrugging.

exercises.name: Standing Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.cue: Compresses the spine under fatigue — the seated, supported dumbbell press trains the same pattern without that cost.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.cue: Lead with the elbows; stop at shoulder height — higher is traps, not delts.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.cue: Constant tension from the bottom — the first 30° is where the cable earns its place.

exercises.name: Machine Lateral Raise
exercises.cue: Pads at the elbows; push out, not up.

exercises.name: Reverse Pec Deck
exercises.cue: Arms sweep back in a wide arc; pause when the hands pass the shoulders.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Rear Delt Fly
exercises.cue: Chest on an incline bench; float the arms out with loose elbows.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.cue: To eye level, alternating arms; no swing.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
exercises.cue: Elbows pinned to the sides; control the lowering — that half builds most of the arm.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.cue: Supinate as you lift; finish with the palm fully up.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.cue: Bench at 60°; the arms hang behind the torso to load the long head at stretch.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.cue: Neutral grip; drive the knuckles up.

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.cue: Tension at the bottom is the point — full extension every rep.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.cue: The pad removes the cheat; lower until the arm is nearly straight.

exercises.name: Cable Pressdown
exercises.cue: Elbows glued to the ribs; only the forearm moves.

exercises.name: Overhead Cable Extension
exercises.cue: Overhead puts the long head on stretch; keep the ribs down as you extend.

exercises.name: Skull Crusher
exercises.cue: Lower the bar behind the head, not to the forehead — better stretch, kinder elbows.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
exercises.cue: Hands just inside shoulder width; elbows track close to the body.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Wrist Curl
exercises.cue: Forearms on the bench; let the weight roll to the fingers, curl back up.

exercises.name: Reverse Curl
exercises.cue: Overhand grip, strict; keep the wrists straight.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.cue: The back stays flat on the pad — the machine takes the spinal load a barbell squat would put on you; stop just before the hips curl.

exercises.name: Belt Squat
exercises.cue: Load hangs from the hips, the spine stays tall and unloaded — squat as deep as feels clean.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.cue: Shoulder pads still compress the spine — the leg press or belt squat covers the same pattern without it.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.cue: The classic — but the bar sits on the spine. Leg press, belt squat, or split squats train the same movement without axial load.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.cue: One moderate dumbbell at the chest; elbows track inside the knees. A pattern builder, not a max-out lift.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.cue: Rear foot on the bench, torso tall; drop the back knee straight down. Dumbbells at the sides keep the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Split Squat
exercises.cue: Same pattern, no load — perfect for volume days and warm-ups.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.cue: Step long enough that the front shin stays vertical; push through the whole foot.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up
exercises.cue: Box at knee height; drive through the top leg, don't push off the floor foot.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.cue: Toes up, squeeze hard at the top; control the descent.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.cue: The bar sits on the hips, not the spine — the flagship spine-safe heavy lift. Ribs down, full lockout, one-second squeeze.

exercises.name: Machine Hip Thrust
exercises.cue: Same pattern, faster setup; drive the hips to a full lockout.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.cue: Seated beats lying for growth — the hip angle keeps the muscle long; pull through the full range.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.cue: Hips stay pinned to the pad; curl to full flexion.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.cue: A loaded hinge on a fatigued spine — the seated leg curl plus hip thrust pair covers hamstrings and glutes without that demand.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.cue: Off the menu under a spine-conscious plan — hip thrusts for the hinge strength, curls for the hamstrings.

exercises.name: Back Extension (Bodyweight)
exercises.cue: Bodyweight only, glute-focused: hinge at the hips, squeeze at the top. Adding load changes the math — don't.

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.cue: A gentler loaded hinge — available only when the spine constraint is off; otherwise the hip thrust owns this slot.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.cue: A ballistic hinge — powerful, but it lives on the far side of the spine filter.

exercises.name: Cable Glute Kickback
exercises.cue: Slight forward lean, kick straight back; the squeeze is the rep.

exercises.name: Hip Abduction Machine
exercises.cue: Push out against the pads and pause; this fills in upper-glute width.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.cue: Knees bent biases the soleus; full stretch at the bottom, pause at the top.

exercises.name: Leg Press Calf Raise
exercises.cue: Straight legs on the sled; the platform lets a full stretch happen safely.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise (Machine)
exercises.cue: Shoulder-pad versions compress the spine — the leg-press version trains the same range without it.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
exercises.cue: Off a step, full range, slow tempo; hold something for balance, not assistance.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.cue: Squeeze the glutes, tuck the ribs; the goal is a straight, braced line — not time for its own sake.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.cue: The lower back stays pressed into the floor while the opposite arm and leg reach away.

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.cue: Press the handle straight out and refuse the twist; the cable is trying to rotate you — don't let it.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.cue: Straight line from ear to ankle; push the floor away from the supporting shoulder.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.cue: Reach long, not high; the hips stay level like a table.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.cue: Roll only as far as the lower back can stay flat; ribs down the whole way.

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.cue: Hanging decompresses the spine; curl the knees up under control, no swing.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.cue: Loaded spinal flexion — exactly what the framework avoids. Anti-movement work covers the midsection instead.

exercises.name: Weighted Sit-Up
exercises.cue: Flexing the spine under load buys ab soreness at a spinal cost — dead bugs and rollouts win.

exercises.name: Sled Push
exercises.cue: Horizontal drive with a neutral spine — the gold-standard spine-safe conditioning piece. Low handles, long strides.

exercises.name: Assault Bike Intervals
exercises.cue: Hard 20-30 second pushes, easy spins between; the fan doesn't lie.

exercises.name: Incline Treadmill Walk
exercises.cue: 12-15% grade, brisk pace, no hands on the rails.

exercises.name: Shuttle Runs
exercises.cue: Test-day specificity: hard accelerations, controlled decelerations, touch the line.

exercises.name: Jump Rope
exercises.cue: Light, quick contacts; it's rhythm practice as much as conditioning.

exercises.name: Rowing Machine
exercises.cue: Legs, body, arms — then reverse; keep the spine long, the hinge happens at the hips.

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry
exercises.cue: A great builder, but it loads the spine axially — sled work covers conditioning without it.
